item.title: Police investigate claims of assault and death of Kinoya man after a joint raid
item.description: Police are currently investigating the death of a man in Velau Drive, Kinoya who was allegedly beaten up during a joint raid last week. They are trying to establish the circumstances surrounding the man's death.
item.content:encoded:
Police are currently investigating the death of a man in Velau Drive, Kinoya who was allegedly beaten up during a joint raid last week.

They are trying to establish the circumstances surrounding the man's death.

It is alleged that the man was assaulted during a raid at his home.

Police confirm an investigation has been directed into the death of the 30-year-old man at the Valelevu Health Centre that was referred to Police by medical officials.

The Criminal Investigations Department will head the investigation.

A post mortem has also been directed to confirm the cause of death.

Initial information gathered from medical officials is that the man had presented himself at the Valelevu Health Centre on Wednesday and was advised that he would need to be admitted to allow for the conduct of further medical tests.

He however signed a discharge slip and returned to his Kinoya home.

Last night, he returned to the health centre, accompanied by his family, as his condition had deteriorated.

Attempts made by health officials to treat his condition were futile.

The information gathered at these initial stages of inquiries are subject to further investigation.

Police say the investigation is also looking into claims that members of the Joint Police and Military Task Force were involved in a raid where the man was present.

item.title: PS Environment Dr Sivendra Michael to appear on a special fijivillage Straight Talk at 8.30pm tonight
item.description: Permanent Secretary for Environment and Climate Change, Doctor Sivendra Michael will appear on a special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan at 8.30pm tonight. The show will focus on the rejection of the Environmental Impact Assessment Report of the proposed Waste to Energy Plant and Port in Vuda submitted by The Next Generation (Fiji) Limited, the concerning issues in the EIA Report, the
item.content:encoded:
Permanent Secretary for Environment and Climate Change, Doctor Sivendra Michael will appear on a special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan at 8.30pm tonight.

The show will focus on the rejection of the Environmental Impact Assessment Report of the proposed Waste to Energy Plant and Port in Vuda submitted by The Next Generation (Fiji) Limited, the concerning issues in the EIA Report, the issues surrounding the environmental concerns, and the work carried out by the Ministry amongst other related issues.

You can watch the show on our facebook page, YouTube channel, TikTok and X account, fijivillage at 8.30pm tonight.

You can also catch the podcast, fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.

Click here for more stories on the TNG Waste to energy incinerator Vuda

item.title: Govt working to minimise fuel crisis impact ahead of crushing season - Tunabuna
item.description: The Government is working to minimise the impact of the ongoing fuel crisis on harvesting, transportation and mill operations as p

item.title: State relying on DNA evidence in QEB armoury break in
item.description: The man who is alleged to have broken into the Queen Elizabeth Barracks armoury made his first appearance in the Suva High Court this morning. Isaia Ledua is charged with one count of Aggravated Burglary and one count of Damaging Property with the intention of entering the RFMF armoury.
item.content:encoded:
The man who is alleged to have broken into the Queen Elizabeth Barracks armoury made his first appearance in the Suva High Court this morning.

Isaia Ledua is charged with one count of Aggravated Burglary and one count of Damaging Property with the intention of entering the RFMF armoury.

Ledua appeared before High Court Judge Justice Daniel Goundar.

He is represented by Jerry Dinati, while the State is represented by Simione Seruvatu.

Seruvatu told the court that there is no CCTV video and they will be relying on DNA evidence moving forward.

He says DNA evidence obtained from the minor component of axe and keypad was similar to the DNA given by the accused.

The State is seeking 14 days to file and serve Information and Disclosures.

Justice Goundar has transferred the case to Justice Dane Tuiqereqere where Ledua's plea will be taken on 22nd June.

item.title: Advanced notice needed on fuel price announcements to avoid market panic - Consumer Council
item.description: The Consumer Council of Fiji is calling for an urgent review of the current fuel price announcement process, saying the "last-minu

item.title: 4-year-old among youngest stroke patients recorded in Fiji
item.description: The youngest recorded stroke patient in Fiji was a four-year-old child, raising concerns among health experts about the increasing number of stroke cases across all age groups, including children and young adults. This was highlighted by Counterstroke Fiji President Elizabeth Reade Fong during fijivillage Straight Talk with Vijay Narayan .
item.content:encoded:
The youngest recorded stroke patient in Fiji was a four-year-old child, raising concerns among health experts about the increasing number of stroke cases across all age groups, including children and young adults.

This was highlighted by Counterstroke Fiji President Elizabeth Reade Fong during fijivillage Straight Talk with Vijay Narayan .

Fong says the four-year-old suffered a stroke following trauma.

There has been a case involving a 19-year-old stroke patient, and one of the patients suffered a stroke at the age of 21 while she was eight months pregnant.

Fong also highlighted the case of a 42-year-old disciplined services officer who suffered a stroke.

She says strokes, once largely associated with older people, are now being diagnosed in much younger individuals, including those in their 20s and 30s.

Insert: Elizabeth on statistics 4th June

Fong believes lifestyle factors are playing a significant role in this trend, pointing to irregular sleep patterns, long working hours and a lack of physical activity as key contributors.

She says Counterstroke Fiji advocates a balanced lifestyle, including eight hours of sleep, eight hours of work and eight hours for rest, family and personal activities, as one way of improving overall wellbeing and reducing the risk of stroke.

Fong says people should also make time for sport, family commitments and community activities as part of maintaining a healthy lifestyle.

item.title: Students to benefit from new HIV and drug awareness materials
item.description: Students across Fiji are expected to benefit from new educational materials aimed at raising awareness about HIV prevention, drug abuse and healthy lifestyle choices. The materials were launched by Assistant Minister for Health and Medical Services Penioni Ravunawa at Delainamasi Government School under the "Na Bula E Dua: Empowering Students with HIV and Drug Use Information Project".
item.content:encoded:
Students across Fiji are expected to benefit from new educational materials aimed at raising awareness about HIV prevention, drug abuse and healthy lifestyle choices.

The materials were launched by Assistant Minister for Health and Medical Services Penioni Ravunawa at Delainamasi Government School under the "Na Bula E Dua: Empowering Students with HIV and Drug Use Information Project".

Speaking during the launch, Ravunawa revealed that Fiji recorded 2,016 new HIV cases in 2025, a 27 percent increase from the previous year, with many infections involving people aged between 15 and 34.

Ravunawa says research continues to show a strong link between unsafe injecting drug use and the spread of HIV.

He also revealed that 117 people, including children, died from HIV-related causes last year.

The Assistant Minister is urging students to avoid peer pressure and focus on their education, sports, faith and healthy living.

He says addressing drug abuse and HIV requires support from parents, schools, communities, faith-based organisations and government agencies.

Meanwhile, Medical Services Pacific says the project is supported by The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints and aims to provide students, teachers and parents with accurate information on HIV prevention, drug awareness and healthy life choices.

The launch was held as part of International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking Week celebrations.

item.title: South Taveuni students educated on fire safety and drug awareness
item.description: Students and teachers at South Taveuni Primary School learned about drug awareness and fire safety during a special programme held as part of Drugs Awareness Week. The demonstrations included a fire drill, safe practices when working at heights, and the proper use of portable fire extinguishers and fire blankets to put out oil fires.
item.content:encoded:
Students and teachers at South Taveuni Primary School learned about drug awareness and fire safety during a special programme held as part of Drugs Awareness Week.

The demonstrations included a fire drill, safe practices when working at heights, and the proper use of portable fire extinguishers and fire blankets to put out oil fires.

The Authority says the programme provided students with valuable lessons on the dangers of drugs and the importance of fire safety, encouraging them to make safe and responsible choices.

They says community outreach and public education continue to play an important role in helping build safer and more resilient communities across Fiji.

item.description: A methamphetamine and marijuana possession case against former Suva lawyer Shazran Abdul Lateef could be dismissed after both the
